verses.text: After the plague, the LORD to Moses and Eleazar the priest: take the sum of all the congregation, from twenty years old and upward, all that are able to go to war. Moses and Eleazar spake to them in the plains of Moab by Jordan near Jericho. Guidance: After plague, a head count. Not to mourn what was lost, but to inventory what is left for the land. The first census forty years ago numbered the doomed; this one numbers the heirs. A new generation, counted at the edge of promise.

After the plague, the LORD to Moses and Eleazar the priest: take the sum of all the congregation, from twenty years old and upward, all that are able to go to war. Moses and Eleazar spake to them in the plains of Moab by Jordan near Jericho. Guidance: After plague, a head count. Not to mourn what was lost, but to inventory what is left for the land. The first census forty years ago numbered the doomed; this one numbers the heirs. A new generation, counted at the edge of promise.

After the plague, the LORD to Moses and Eleazar the priest: take the sum of all the congregation, from twenty years old and upward, all that are able to go to war. Moses and Eleazar spake to them in the plains of Moab by Jordan near Jericho. Guidance: After plague, a head count. Not to mourn what was lost, but to inventory what is left for the land. The first census forty years ago numbered the doomed; this one numbers the heirs. A new generation, counted at the edge of promise.

After the plague, the LORD to Moses and Eleazar the priest: take the sum of all the congregation, from twenty years old and upward, all that are able to go to war. Moses and Eleazar spake to them in the plains of Moab by Jordan near Jericho. Guidance: After plague, a head count. Not to mourn what was lost, but to inventory what is left for the land. The first census forty years ago numbered the doomed; this one numbers the heirs. A new generation, counted at the edge of promise.

The tribes are counted: Reuben 43,730; Simeon collapsed to 22,200; Judah largest at 76,500; Benjamin, Dan, Asher, Naphtali, Manasseh, Ephraim — all written by family. Korah's sons did not die. Zelophehad's five daughters are noted by name: Mahlah, Noah, Hoglah, Milcah, Tirzah. Guidance: God remembers names. Simeon's collapse may mark Peor's price. Korah's line survives — children are not condemned for fathers. Zelophehad's daughters get a line in the genealogy, foreshadowing the inheritance question to come. No woman is invisible to heaven.

All numbered: 601,730. The LORD: unto these the land shall be divided for an inheritance by the number of names. To many give a larger inheritance, to few a smaller; every one shall be given according to those that were numbered. By lot shall the land be divided. Guidance: God's math is fair, not flat. Larger families get larger plots, smaller families smaller — but the LOT decides where, not the politicking. The land is gift before it is geography. No one gets to choose their corner; every corner is given.

To many give a larger inheritance, to few a smaller; every one shall be given according to those that were numbered. By lot shall the land be divided. Guidance: God's math is fair, not flat. Larger families get larger plots, smaller families smaller — but the LOT decides where, not the politicking. The land is gift before it is geography. No one gets to choose their corner; every corner is given.

These were the Levites: Gershon, Kohath, Merari — their families numbered. Of Kohath came Amram, whose wife bare Aaron, Moses, and Miriam. Aaron begat Nadab and Abihu — who died offering strange fire. All Levite males from a month old: 23,000. Not numbered with Israel — they had no inheritance. Guidance: The Levites carry the tabernacle but own no plot. Their inheritance is the LORD. The text quietly reminds us of Nadab and Abihu, who treated the holy lightly. The lesson is not buried — it is written into the family tree.

These were numbered by Moses and Eleazar in the plains of Moab. But among these there was not a man whom Moses and Aaron numbered in the wilderness of Sinai — for the LORD had said, they shall surely die in the wilderness. There was not left a man, save Caleb son of Jephunneh and Joshua son of Nun. Guidance: Forty years vindicated. Two men crossed the line of the spies' refusal, and two men stand here from that first count. God's word, however slow, finishes itself. Caleb and Joshua live to see the day their generation said could never come.
